name: Skyline Gondola & Luge
tagline: Queenstown's most iconic family activity — gondola up, luge down
summary: Take the gondola up Bob's Peak for panoramic views of Queenstown and Lake Wakatipu, then ride the luge down. Kids can ride solo from age 3. The restaurant at the top is good value for lunch. Budget 2–4 hours.

price.note: Luge rides are additional ($16–30/ride or buy a multi-ride pass)

tips: Go mid-week to avoid queues
Buy multi-ride luge passes for better value
The restaurant has a kids menu and great views

name: Te Puia
tagline: Geysers, kiwi birds, and living Maori culture in one park
summary: Te Puia is Rotorua's must-do. The Pohutu Geyser (NZ's largest) erupts almost every hour, there's a kiwi house with live kiwi birds, and a working Maori arts school. Evening cultural performances available. Genuinely world-class.
tips: Go at 10:15am to catch the Pohutu Geyser eruption cycle
The kiwi house is dark inside — let kids' eyes adjust
Book evening hangi separately

name: Wai-O-Tapu Thermal Wonderland
tagline: Alien-coloured thermal pools that look like they're from another planet
summary: Wai-O-Tapu is 30km south of Rotorua and features some of the most colourful geothermal pools in the world. The Lady Knox Geyser erupts at 10:15am daily (artificially triggered with soap). Allow 1.5–2 hours for the walking circuit. Kids are transfixed.
tips: Arrive before 10:15am for the Lady Knox Geyser
The Champagne Pool is the star attraction
Combine with Rainbow Springs on the same day

name: Agrodome Farm Show
tagline: NZ farm life made fun — sheepdogs, sheep shearing, and bottle-feeding lambs
summary: The Agrodome is a working sheep and cattle farm with daily 1-hour shows. Kids get to feed lambs, watch sheep being shorn, and see sheepdogs work. Fully undercover, great for rainy days. One of Rotorua's most-loved family activities for over 50 years.
tips: Shows run at 9:30am, 11am, and 2:30pm
Arrive 15 mins early for front row seats
The 9:30am show is least crowded

name: International Antarctic Centre
tagline: Experience Antarctica — without actually going — in a world-class attraction
summary: The International Antarctic Centre is one of NZ's best family attractions. You can experience a simulated -18°C Antarctic storm, hand-feed Little Blue Penguins, ride a Hagglund all-terrain vehicle, and explore interactive exhibits about Scott's Terra Nova expedition. Plan 3+ hours.

price.note: Penguin feeding at set times — check schedule

tips: Book penguin feeding time in advance — limited capacity
The storm room is intense but fun — toddlers may find it scary
Great first-day activity before picking up your rental car

name: Huka Falls
tagline: New Zealand's most visited natural attraction — and it's completely free
summary: The Waikato River narrows to just 15m wide at Huka Falls, creating a turquoise torrent of water. 220,000 litres per second crash through the canyon. It's an easy 10-minute walk from the carpark and genuinely spectacular. Kids are mesmerised. Totally free.
tips: Park at the Huka Falls carpark (free)
The pedestrian bridge gives the best view
Combine with the Huka Jet boat experience next door

name: Huka Jet Boat
tagline: NZ's most thrilling 30 minutes — jet boat through a river gorge at full speed
summary: The Huka Jet runs from the base of Huka Falls and blasts through the Waikato River gorge at up to 85km/h, executing 360-degree spins. Kids from 3 are welcome (and love it). Minimum height 90cm. Book ahead in summer — it sells out daily.

price.note: Combo deals with Huka Falls walk available

tips: Book online for 10% discount
Wear clothes you don't mind getting slightly wet
Go in the morning — afternoon wind can make it rougher

name: Waitangi Treaty Grounds
tagline: Where NZ was founded — and one of the most important historic sites in the country
summary: Waitangi is where the Treaty of Waitangi was signed in 1840, making it NZ's founding document site. The museum is world-class, there's a daily Maori cultural performance, and the grounds overlook the Bay of Islands beautifully. Under-15s are free.
tips: The free cultural performance runs multiple times daily
Kids under 8 may find the history less engaging — do 1 hour and focus on the canoe and performance
The grounds cafe has great coffee

name: Dolphin Watching Cruise
tagline: Wild dolphin encounters in the Bay of Islands — a bucket-list experience for kids
summary: The Bay of Islands has a resident pod of bottlenose dolphins and frequent visitors including common dolphins. Most operators offer a swim-with option (if dolphins cooperate) and a watch-from-the-boat option. Sightings are near-guaranteed year-round. One of NZ's best wildlife experiences.

price.note: Swimming upgrade available, boat trip guaranteed but swimming depends on dolphin behaviour

tips: Book 2+ weeks ahead in December–January
Bring sea-sickness tablets if sensitive
Morning cruises tend to have calmer seas

name: Puzzling World
tagline: Wanaka's quirkiest attraction — illusions, mazes, and mind-bending puzzle rooms
summary: Puzzling World is a Wanaka institution. The 3D illusion rooms are genuinely mind-bending, the holographic gallery is eerie and fun, and the outdoor maze takes 30–60 minutes to complete. Great for all ages — adults enjoy it as much as kids. Partially rain-safe.

price.note: Maze and illusion rooms combined ticket is best value

tips: The illusion rooms are the highlight — budget 45 mins
The maze has a 'cheat route' for young kids who get frustrated
Combine with lunch at Pembroke Patisserie nearby

name: Christchurch Gondola
tagline: 360° views of Christchurch, Banks Peninsula, and the Southern Alps
summary: The Christchurch Gondola takes you to the rim of an extinct volcano above the city for panoramic views. On a clear day you can see from the Alps to the sea. The Time Tunnel ride inside gives kids a fun intro to Canterbury's history. Allow 1.5 hours.
tips: Go on a clear day for best views — Mt Cook visible from the top
The Time Tunnel ride is free with gondola ticket
Walking tracks at the top offer extra exploration

name: Orana Wildlife Park
tagline: NZ's only open-range zoo — where giraffes eat from your hand
summary: Orana Park is NZ's only open-range zoo and one of the best in the Southern Hemisphere. You ride through the African Savannah enclosure by bus while lions, giraffes, and rhinos roam alongside. Hand-feed the giraffes at 2pm daily. Excellent native wildlife section too.
tips: Giraffe feeding at 2pm is the highlight — don't miss it
The native wildlife section often has kiwi activity at dusk
Bring a picnic — the grounds are large and beautiful
